2605.04886.title: BenCSSmark: Making the Social Sciences Count in LLM Research
2605.04886.paper_summary: This position paper argues that the under-representation of social science tasks in contemporary LLM benchmarks limits advances in both LLM evaluation and social scientific inquiry. Benchmarks -- standardized tools for assessing computational systems -- are pivotal in the development of artificial intelligence (AI), including large language models (LLMs). Benchmarks do more than measure progress -- they actively structure it, shaping reputations, research agendas, and commercial outcomes. Despite this central role, the social sciences are largely absent from mainstream evaluation frameworks, even though scholars in these fields generate dozens of rigorously annotated, context-sensitive datasets each year. Integrating this work into benchmark design could significantly improve the generalization and robustness of AI models. In turn, models trained on social scientific tasks would likely yield better performance on classic and contemporary tasks in disciplines as diverse as history, sociology, political science or economics. This is all the more pressing as these disciplines are quickly turning to LLMs for assistance. To address this gap, we introduce BenCSSmark, a benchmark composed of datasets annotated by computational social scientists. By integrating social scientific perspectives into benchmarking, BenCSSmark seeks to promote more robust, transparent, and socially relevant AI systems and to foster efficient collaboration.
2605.04886.paper_summary_zh: 這份立場文件主張，當代大型語言模型（LLM）基準中社會科學任務的不足代表性限制了LLM評估和社會科學探究的進展。基準——評估計算系統的標準化工具——在人工智慧（AI）發展中至關重要，包括大型語言模型（LLMs）。基準不僅僅是衡量進展——它們還積極地結構化進展，塑造聲譽、研究議程和商業結果。儘管這一中心角色，社會科學在主流評估框架中幾乎缺席，儘管這些領域的學者每年生成數十個經過嚴格註釋的、具上下文敏感性的數據集。將這些工作整合到基準設計中可以顯著改善AI模型的泛化能力和穩健性。反過來，基於社會科學任務訓練的模型，可能在歷史、社會學、政治學或經濟學等多樣化學科的經典和當代任務上表現更佳。隨著這些學科迅速轉向LLMs以獲取幫助，這一問題顯得更加迫切。為了解決這一差距，我們介紹了BenCSSmark，一個由計算社會科學家註釋的數據集組成的基準。通過將社會科學視角整合到基準評估中，BenCSSmark旨在促進更穩健、透明和社會相關的AI系統，並促進高效的合作。
2605.04886.author: Arnault Chatelain et.al.
2605.04886.authors: Arnault Chatelain, Étienne Ollion, Qianwen Guan, Diandra Fabre, Lorraine Goeuriot, Emile Chapuis, Abdelkrim Beloued, Marie Candito, Nicolas Hervé, Didier Schwab
